channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Health Dept. Rescinds Freeze on $10 Billion for 5 Democratic States
item.description: The reversal of the funding pause came after the administration faced repeated setbacks in a suit challenging the decision, which could have jeopardized programs for low-income households.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.media:description: Last week, the Health and Human Services Department sent letters to five states saying that it was voiding measures to limit the states’ access to federal funding for child care and social services.

item.title: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
item.description: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold and Carl Hulse
item.media:description: A lowered flag at the U.S. Capitol on Monday in honor of Senator Lindsey Graham, who died over the weekend.

item.title: Democratic Senate Candidates in Maine Denounce Deadly ICE Shooting
item.description: The killing of a man in Biddeford quickly became an issue in the Senate race, with Democrats aiming to tie Senator Susan Collins to President Trump’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:description: Protesters in Biddeford, Maine, outside Senator Susan Collins’s office after the fatal shooting of a man by an ICE agent in the city on Monday.

item.title: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
item.description: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: A Guide to the Uncanny 2026 Midterm Elections, and How We Got Here
item.description: A lot of things have changed in American politics since our writer went on parental leave.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
item.description: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Sean Rayford for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, is the Democratic Senate candidate in South Carolina.

item.title: Trump Administration Announces New Attack on the International Criminal Court
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the United States will “dismantle” the international court that tries grave crimes “brick by brick, if necessary.”
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek, Amanda Taub and Edward Wong
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s attack on the International Criminal Court is unlike any before.

item.title: Trump’s Threat to Impose Fees in the Strait of Hormuz Contradicts His Aides
item.description: President Trump said the United States could collect tolls or fees, despite Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio saying no country could do so.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships sailing near the Strait of Hormuz off the eastern coast of the United Arab Emirates at Khor Fakkan on Monday.

item.title: How Lindsey Graham’s Journey From Trump Critic to Ally Made Him a Power Player
item.description: He initially called Donald Trump a “kook” who was “unfit for office,” only to join the new president’s Mar-a-Lago circle. “I’m still in the game,” Mr. Graham once explained of his much-analyzed turnabout.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:description: “There’s no way to get around Trump,” Senator Lindsey Graham said years ago. “He’s the most important figure in the Republican Party now.”

item.title: Judge Denounces Trump’s I.R.S. Suit as Improper Exercise in Self-Dealing
item.description: The decision also recommended disciplinary action against the lawyers involved, including the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Internal Revenue Service office building in Washington. President Trump sued the I.R.S. in January, blaming it for the leak of his tax returns during his first term.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Appointed to Finish His Senate Term After Trump’s Backing
item.description: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina named Darline Graham Nordone to replace Mr. Graham, who died on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

item.title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
item.description: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Gavin McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Bojangles in Savannah, Ga., recently added E.V. charging stations outside the restaurant.

item.title: Mercurial and Magnetic, Lindsey Graham Was a Force in the Senate
item.description: The sharp-witted South Carolinian repaired relations with President Trump to advance his foreign policy goals, was willing to cut deals with Democrats and tried to insert himself into every legislative fight.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham during a bill signing in the Oval Office in February. He once called Donald J. Trump a “demagogue,” but fully ingratiated himself after Mr. Trump became president.

item.title: Maine Democrats to Chuck Schumer: Stay Out of Our Senate Race
item.description: Local Democrats are warning the top Senate Democrat to keep away as they replace Graham Platner, and the candidates are giving his leadership in Washington low marks.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Nathan Howard for The New York Times
item.media:description: Many Democrats in Maine don’t want Senator Chuck Schumer of New York to be involved as they pick a replacement Senate nominee.

item.title: Inside Israel’s Secret Operation to Install Ahmadinejad, Iran’s Former President, as Leader
item.description: The yearslong effort to groom the former Iranian president as an intelligence asset culminated in a dramatic effort to take him to a Mossad safe house in Iran in the early days of the war. But the plan fell apart.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, Iran’s former leader, was known for accelerating Iran’s nuclear program and his anti-Israel views.

item.title: With Threats Rising, Supreme Court Asks Congress to Increase Security Funds
item.description: Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are scheduled to make a rare appearance at the Capitol to testify about the court’s more than $200 million request.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: Police outside the Supreme Court in Washington earlier this year.

item.title: After Weeks of Silence, McConnell Says He Is Recovering From a Fall
item.description: The Republican senator denied that he had suffered a heart attack and said he had left the hospital and moved to a physical rehabilitation center. He did not give a timetable for returning to Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz
item.media:description: Senator Mitch McConnell on Capitol Hill earlier this year.

item.title: With Lindsey Graham Gone, Ukraine Loses an Ally in Trump’s Ear
item.description: The South Carolina Republican was Ukraine’s most influential champion inside President Trump’s mostly “America First” political orbit.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Senator Graham during a news conference after a meeting with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kyiv on Friday.

item.title: What We Know About Lindsey Graham’s Death
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz
item.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham during an appearance at the BMW manufacturing facility in Spartanburg, S.C., last month.

item.title: Michael Cohen Helped Convict Trump. Now, He’s Making Nice Again.
item.description: A previously unreported encounter last summer set the stage for a rapprochement between the president and his former fixer, who has so far avoided the diatribes and prosecutions that President Trump has directed at other critics.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Ben Protess
item.category: New York State Criminal Case Against Trump (71543-23)
item.media:description: Michael Cohen outside a Manhattan courtroom in 2023. He testified in Mr. Trump’s criminal trial that he was directed to falsify records by the president, who was ultimately convicted on 34 counts.
